title: Template literal URL with a number placeholder
description: mion templateLiteral.spec.ts 'URL pattern api/user/${number}'. Compiled to `^api\/user\/-?(?:\d+\.?\d*|\.\d+)$` at RT-build time; validate emits `typeof v === 'string' && regex.test(v)`.
notes: Template literal types are compiled to a JS RegExp at build time and matched at runtime with `regex.test(v)`.
The `${number}` placeholder expects digit-strings (`42`, `-7`, `3.14`) — NOT the words "NaN" or "Infinity" even though those are typeof "number" at the JS level.
